pattern: Detecting whether the input is an email or a phone number, then applying the fixed masking rule for that form, reduces the task to two independent string rewrites.
hints: An at-sign tells you it is an email; otherwise it is a phone number to be stripped of separators.
For an email, lowercase everything and keep only the first and last letters of the name with five asterisks between them.
For a phone, keep only the digits; the trailing ten are the local number and any leading digits are the country code, which selects one of the four fixed output templates.

edge_inputs.description: first example, mixed-case email name and domain
second example, two-letter name still gets five asterisks
third example, ten-digit phone with separators
plain ten-digit phone, zero country code
phone with a one-digit country code and plus sign

stress.description: alternating random emails and phone numbers within the length bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'
        name_len = rng.randint(2, 8)
        name = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(name_len))
        dom_a = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(rng.randint(2, 6)))
        dom_b = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(rng.randint(2, 3)))
        return [name + '@' + dom_a + '.' + dom_b]
    cc = rng.randint(0, 3)
    digits = [str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(10 + cc)]
    seps = ['+', '-', '(', ')', ' ']
    out = ''
    if cc > 0:
        out += '+'
    for i, d in enumerate(digits):
        out += d
        if i < len(digits) - 1 and rng.random() < 0.3:
            out += rng.choice(seps)
    return [out[:20]]
stress.note: generated forms stay valid emails or phones so masking is well defined

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maskPII(self, s):
        if '@' in s:
            t = s.lower()
            at = t.index('@')
            name = t[:at]
            rest = t[at:]
            return name[0] + '*****' + name[-1] + rest
        digits = ''.join(c for c in s if c.isdigit())
        local = digits[-10:]
        cc = len(digits) - 10
        tail = '***-***-' + local[-4:]
        if cc == 0:
            return tail
        return '+' + ('*' * cc) + '-' + tail

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maskPII(self, s):
        if '@' in s:
            s = s.lower()
            name, domain = s.split('@', 1)
            masked = name[0] + '*****' + name[-1]
            return masked + '@' + domain
        digits = [c for c in s if c.isdigit()]
        n = len(digits)
        country = n - 10
        last4 = ''.join(digits[-4:])
        base = '***-***-' + last4
        prefixes = {0: '', 1: '+*-', 2: '+**-', 3: '+***-'}
        return prefixes[country] + base
